text: Agile methodology is a popular way to manage projects, especially in software development. Instead of planning everything at the start, teams work in short cycles called **sprints**, which usually last one to two weeks. Each sprint begins with a planning session where the team selects tasks from a **backlog**—a list of all the work needed. These tasks are often described as **user stories**, which explain a feature from the perspective of the end user. During the sprint, the team holds daily **standups**—short meetings to share progress and discuss any problems. After each sprint, the team reviews the work and plans the next one. This **iterative development** approach allows teams to adjust quickly and deliver value step by step.
